# commit pick --no-restack leaves upstack branches untouched.

[!git:2.45.0] skip # feature requires git 2.45

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-07-12T12:30:00Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init
git config spice.experiment.commitPick true

cp $WORK/extra/source.txt source.txt
git add source.txt
gs branch create source -m 'Add source'
gs trunk
git add target.txt
gs branch create target -m 'Add target'
git add upstack1.txt
gs branch create upstack1 -m 'Add upstack1'
git add upstack2.txt
gs branch create upstack2 -m 'Add upstack2'

git rev-parse --short upstack1
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/upstack1-before.txt
git rev-parse --short upstack2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/upstack2-before.txt

# Pick onto the bottom branch, where automatic restacking would replay both
# upstack branches.
gs bottom
gs commit pick --no-restack source

# Both original heads prove that neither upstack branch was replayed.
git rev-parse --short upstack1
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/upstack1-before.txt
git rev-parse --short upstack2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/upstack2-before.txt

# The picked change must still be applied even though restacking was skipped.
cmp source.txt $WORK/extra/source.txt

-- extra/source.txt --
Source
-- repo/target.txt --
Target
-- repo/upstack1.txt --
Upstack 1
-- repo/upstack2.txt --
Upstack 2
-- golden/upstack1-before.txt --
107d302
-- golden/upstack2-before.txt --
9ae7aa6
